name: Smoke Testing

x.defectClass: Catastrophic, obvious breakage — the build does not start, core paths are dead.
x.oracle: A small set of critical "does it even work" checks with known expected outcomes.
x.automationLevel: High — a fast automated suite run on every build/deploy.
blindSpots: Shallow: misses everything beyond the happy path
x.lifecycleStage: CI gate / post-deploy verification (fast, first line).

def: A quick, shallow set of checks confirming that the most critical functions of a build work at all, before deeper testing or release proceeds.
problem: Running full test suites or promoting a fundamentally broken build wastes time; you want a fast "is it dead on arrival?" gate.
mechanism: A small suite exercises the few must-work paths (app boots, login works, key endpoint responds). It runs first; failure stops the pipeline / blocks deeper testing — the term comes from powering on hardware to see if it smokes.
limits: Must be kept fast and stable
failures: Smoke suite growing slow / flaky and losing its purpose
Trusting green smoke as release quality
whenNot: As a substitute for thorough testing
When deep verification is what is actually needed
myths: "Smoke tests prove it works" — they prove it is not obviously broken
